TextENGNew: "Woohoo, fast, fast! The cars are zooming by! Ahahaha!"
In the passenger seat, Miyuki kicked her arms and legs about, gazing at the passing scenery outside with unceasing wonder.
At least the seatbelt was keeping her from running laps around the interior of the car — but how long that happy state of affairs would last, I wasn't terribly confident.
"Hey, Papa. Can I open the window?"
"What's the matter? Have you gone carsick?"
"Nope, I'm fine. I just want to feel the wind outside."
"...The wind?"
"Yeah! Morita-sensei let me ride on her motorbike the other day! Motorbikes are so cool — they're fast, and they look amazing!"
changeReason: New translation specifies Morita-sensei as female ("her motorbike") and changes temporal reference from "that time before" to "the other day".

TextENGNew: "Oh... is that so..."
"When Miyuki grows up, she's going to buy a motorbike! Beautiful girl rider Miyuki Akasaka, flying at full speed down the highway! ...Just kidding, ehehe!"
"...Please be patient a little longer."
...The future suddenly fills me with profound unease. Just who exactly is this Morita-sensei, filling a young child's head with ideas like that?!
changeReason: Old translation used "he" for Morita-sensei; new translation removes gendered pronoun, altering assumed gender.

TextENGNew: And still... the image of a grown-up Miyuki, hair streaming loose, skillfully handling a large motorbike in a car chase with a police cruiser.
...Just imagining it sent an involuntary shudder down my spine, though it certainly wasn't cold.
"You can put your seat back and take a nap, you know, Miyuki. I'll wake you properly when we arrive."
"That's all right! We haven't been on a drive together in so long, Papa. Ahahaha!"
"...I see."
And on the other hand — the sheer sweetness of that reply from my daughter made something rise in my chest, and I felt warmth gather at the corners of my eyes.
So I gripped the wheel firmly, on no account letting myself be the cause of an accident, and kept my eyes squarely on the road ahead.
"Couldn't you just pick up a brochure at a travel agency, or buy a travel magazine? There's really no need to go on a scouting trip in person..."
That's what Yukie had said with a wry smile as she saw me off.
But since this was going to be a family trip, I had a certain stubbornness about getting things right — and so I'd arranged to visit the prospective destination in a single day.
...In truth, the most excited person here might be me.
That said, having Miyuki along for what was meant to be a simple there-and-back journey was a happy miscalculation.
Just then... glancing up at the motorway sign as it passed, I caught a certain place name printed there.
Come to think of it — this was near that town.
The last time I'd come was five years ago. And there, I had... met her...
"...Miyuki. Would it be all right to make a small detour?"
"A detour? Where?"
"A town called Okonomiya. I'd like to call in very briefly to say hello to someone I know there."
changeReason: Town name spelling changed from "Okinomiya" to "Okonomiya".

TextENGNew: Coming off the motorway exit, I drove on from memory of the route.
It wasn't a particularly complicated drive, and I found Okonomiya Police Station without once getting lost, in under an hour.
changeReason: Town name changed from "Okinomiya" to "Okonomiya".

TextENGNew: "There it is, there it is — right, here we go."
I parked in the visitors' lot and got out, leading Miyuki by the hand.
At the front reception of the entrance I gave my name, and was just about to ask for Detective Oishi's whereabouts — when that familiar booming voice called out to me.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Oh? Oh ho! Could that be... Akasaka-san?! Na ha ha ha! How many years has it been?!"
"Oishi-san...! I've been terribly out of touch. I'm glad to see you're well."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Oishi-san clapped a hand firmly on my shoulder and seized the hand I offered, squeezing it heartily in welcome.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "N'huhuh~ Oh come now, 'glad you're well' is a bit unkind! I've just started an apple diet, you see — I had the impression I was trimming down quite nicely!"
"And as for Akasaka-san — you've gone and filled out quite impressively since I last saw you!"
"If I don't keep in at least this kind of shape, my body won't hold together."
"Hear, hear — that's the dependable answer I'd expect! Na ha ha ha!! ...Oh? N'huhuh, and could that be...?"
Oishi-san's gaze shifted to Miyuki, who was standing vaguely at my side, and he crouched down to peer at her face.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: In response, my daughter gave a startled flinch and hid herself behind my back.
"Hey now, Miyuki. Say hello properly."
"...Um, well... er..."
Clutching the hem of my shirt in a tight fist, she eyed Oishi-san's face with a distinctly suspicious expression.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: ...She didn't usually take this long to warm up to strangers — what had gotten into her?
"...I'm sorry. She isn't normally like this..."
"Now, now, it's quite all right. Uncle must have a scary face... Might I be allowed to ask your name, if you don't mind?"
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "...Um, Miyuki Akasaka. Five years old. H-... hello."
"Well done, that's an excellent answer. Just as one would expect from Akasaka-san's daughter... Na ha ha ha!"
Oishi-san stroked Miyuki's head, his face wearing a fond smile like a grandfather's.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Seeing that, Miyuki at last broke into a relieved smile of her own.
"What was that about, Miyuki? You've been very well-behaved today."
"Well... Mama said just the other day to be careful around people I don't know."
"Na ha ha ha!! Indeed, quite right! You've got me there — wahahaha!!"
Far from taking offence, Oishi-san seemed genuinely delighted by this, and shook with laughter.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Good grief... Mama is clearly a truly formidable presence in Miyuki's world.
"Papa... I'm thirsty. Can I go get some water?"
"Wait until we go out for a meal, Miyuki. I'll take you soon."
"Ah, if that's the case — Uncle will treat you to a juice. Here, a hundred yen."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Yaaay!"
"H-hey, Miyuki..."
Miyuki, delighted, accepted the hundred-yen coin from Oishi-san and looked up at me with an expression of gleeful triumph.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Those wide, round eyes and a smile so bright it practically sparkled... Is it a weakness in my character that I can't bring myself to scold her when she looks like that?
"I'm sorry about that, Oishi-san..."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "No, no, no, don't give it a thought! Ah, Miyuki-chan — the drinks vending machine is at the far end of this corridor. Can you manage by yourself?"
"Yes! Thank you, Uncle Oishi!!"
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: With that, Miyuki gave an energetic bow and then went skipping away on light feet.
"N'huhuh~ What a good girl. Being able to say thank you properly — that alone is something to be proud of."
"Not at all... I'm sorry, Oishi-san. My wife was just scolding me the other day for spoiling her too much..."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Na ha ha, can't be helped. They say a father always ends up overprotective when it comes to his daughter."
"...Ah, but what a shame. If you had let me know a little earlier, I'd have arranged not just a drink but a proper reception!"
"I'm sorry... I was just passing through the area today. But I'll come back soon and bring my wife for a proper visit. When we do, please do let us treat you somewhere."
"Not at all! I wouldn't dream of intruding on precious family time~!"
"Oh, by the way — the Watanagashi festival will be taking place soon at Hinamizawa, just next door. If you'd like, please do come along with your family."
"There'll be food stalls and all sorts of festivity — I'm sure your daughter would love it."
"Oh, yes. I'd be delighted to come. ...Oh."
Just then. Just for a moment.
A fragment of an old memory surfaced in my mind.
If I recalled correctly, the last time I had visited Hinamizawa... I had met a little girl.
Her name was... Rika Furude, if I remembered right. How old would she be now...?
"Rika-chan..."
"...Oh? Akasaka-san — you're acquainted with Rika of the Furude family? Well, that's even more convenient. She's the star of the Watanagashi, you know — she'd be very pleased if you came to watch."
"Is... is that so. Is she well?"
"As well as can be — making a big commotion every day with her friends."
"...I'm glad."
Hearing that, I felt a wave of relief. I hadn't imagined any ill had come to her — but knowing she'd grown up healthy and happy was genuinely good to hear.
Come to think of it — hadn't she once said something to me...?
"...Oh, that reminds me — the timing today is perfect! There's someone I absolutely must introduce you to."
"Me... you mean me?"
"Now where has she gone...? She should still be in the building, she hasn't headed back to her precinct yet."
"Nngh... there we go..."

After finishing a meeting in the station chief's office and on her way back to the detective room, Tomoe happened to glance into the break room she was passing — and found a small girl stretching up on her tiptoes toward the vending machine.
"A child...? Whose child could that be?"
This break room was for staff. Which meant the child was not a member of the public, but a child of someone in the police.
But... had she seen such an adorable little girl this morning...?
"Nngh... ah..."
The child seemed to be struggling earnestly to slot the hundred-yen coin in her hand into the coin slot and buy a juice.
But unfortunately, the vending machine here was made with adults in mind, and was a little too tall for a child. For all her valiant effort, she was coming up ten-odd centimetres short.
"What's the matter, little one? Trying to buy a juice?"
"Hm? Oh... yeah!"
"All right, just wait a moment. Up we go."
Unable to watch any further, Tomoe spoke to the girl and then stepped around behind her, lifted her under the arms, and hoisted her up.
The girl's eyes went wide at having her feet suddenly leave the ground, and she turned to look — but Tomoe just smiled brightly.
"That should do it, right? There you go."
She turned back, and right in front of her was the coin slot. The girl put in her hundred yen and pressed the button for the orange juice next to it.
Down at the collection slot at her feet, a can came clattering down.
"There we go, wasn't that easy. Hehe..."
"Yeah! Thank you, 'Auntie~!'"
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: ...Auntie.
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: Auntie... she said?
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: An impulse to, from this very position, take her into a backdrop — that was what Tomoe barely managed to suppress with the last remnants of her reason.
True... to a child's eyes, a woman in her thirties was an "auntie." Getting bent out of shape about it was the childish thing to do.
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "auntie" and "near 30" to "in her thirties", altering the age reference.

TextENGNew: ...Though perhaps simply thinking that was already childish enough.
"...Hey, Auntie? Why are you crying — did you hurt somewhere?"
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: "I-it's nothing. Ah, ahahaha..."
Struggling to keep her contorted face in something resembling a smile, Tomoe wept in her heart: Oh, come on.
At any rate, at least there was no one else in this room. With that thought, she was about to lower the girl back to the floor when —
"...Oh, there you are, Tomoe-chan. Been looking for you."
The door to the break room behind her opened, and two men came inside.
One of them was Oishi — the veteran detective who had looked after her for many years.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: And the other was...
"Now isn't this fortunate. There's someone I absolutely wanted to introduce you to. Akasaka-san, this is Inspector Minaoi of the National Police Agency."
changeReason: Name changed from "Minai" to "Minaoi"; title restructured. "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Akasaka, Superintendent of the Metropolitan Police Department. A pleasure to meet you."
changeReason: Title changed from "Commissioner" to "Superintendent"; different rank rendering.

TextENGNew: "Oh... and the pleasure is mine! I'm Minaoi, Section Chief of the NPA Public Relations Division. It's an honour."
changeReason: Name changed from "Minai" to "Minaoi"; title from "Chief Clerk of Public Relations at NPA" to "Section Chief of the NPA Public Relations Division".

TextENGNew: At the salute of the man called Akasaka — along with his title of Superintendent — Tomoe returned the salute, adroitly keeping the girl balanced in one arm.
changeReason: Title changed from "Commissioner" to "Superintendent" and character name "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: ...But the girl in her arm, apparently wanting to imitate, took up a saluting posture as well — and Tomoe couldn't help letting out a small laugh.
"Well, well... N'huhuhuh! Quite the natural with children — the old youth guidance work shows, I see~!"
"Hm? Oh, th-this isn't..."
"I'm sorry — it seems my daughter has been in your care. Miyuki, have you said your thanks properly?"
"Hmph, of course I did! Right?"
"Ahaha... yes, indeed. What a lovely daughter you have."
Only if she hadn't said 'auntie' — that was the murmur Tomoe swallowed back down, as she gently set the girl — Miyuki — back on the floor.
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: "Tomo-... whoops, Inspector Minaoi and I have been working together on a rather intensive investigation at the moment. Akasaka-san, she's a remarkable officer, I assure you."
changeReason: Name changed from "Minai" to "Minaoi" and title references restructured.

TextENGNew: "Particularly in profiling analysis — she has skill and knowledge that rank among the finest in Japan. She's in public relations now, but originally she was a detective with the Aichi prefectural police."
"You're exaggerating, Oishi-san. Really..."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Oh? Profiling analysis? I've had occasion to read about that investigative technique in foreign papers a few times. To have fixed on something like that — quite remarkable. I do hope you'll favour me with your acquaintance."
"No, no... I'm just a novice. Um — Akasaka-san. If I may ask —"
"Oh? Ah, my introduction was insufficient. I'm with the Public Security Bureau of the Metropolitan Police Department. I owe a great deal to Oishi-san from a previous investigation..."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Listening to Akasaka's answer, Tomoe understood.
Public Security detectives generally preferred not to reveal their affiliations — even to officers from other departments.
His willingness to mention it here indicated that Oishi already knew his identity.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: And the investigation they had supposedly undertaken together was in all likelihood Public Security-related... that was her conclusion.
"My apologies as well. My father was also in Public Security, so I understand the circumstances. Please do trust me."
"...That helps."
And from that response, Akasaka too took the measure of her intelligence. ...As one would expect of someone Oishi was introducing — she was a capable officer.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "By the way... Akasaka-san? You mentioned you were just passing through the area — where are you headed?"
"I stopped in on my way to Wakura Onsen. We've decided to take a family trip to a hot spring..."
"My, how splendid~! The scenery around those hot spring grounds is something else. Your wife and your daughter are sure to love it. Lucky you, isn't it, Miyuki-chan?"
"Yes!"
Clutching her can of orange juice in both hands and drinking it with evident delight, Miyuki gave Oishi an enthusiastic nod.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: All trace of her earlier wariness was gone — she and Oishi together had the warmth of a grandfather and grandchild.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Even so — how fortunate that you managed to get the time off for a family trip. The last time we spoke on the phone, you sounded terribly busy..."
"Well, about that..."
"Oh, forgive me — I'm prying. N'huhuhuh..."
"Not at all, please don't worry."

"To tell the truth, it's rather an embarrassing story — the investigation headquarters I was involved with was dissolved a few days ago on the orders of my superiors. I've been told to take time off as a chance to refresh myself..."
"Oh? Is that so..."
Apparently feeling that a joking response would be unseemly, Oishi walked to the vending machine, bought a coffee, and held it out to Akasaka.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Akasaka thanked him, took it, pulled the tab and took a sip... then, choosing his words with care, gave a brief account of the circumstances.
"...I see. That must have been a difficult time."

"It's a disappointment. We had obtained solid evidence, and had we pressed the attack there was every chance of victory. It was also the case my supervisor in the Prosecutors Office had been pursuing for a long time... I very much wanted to see an arrest made."
"Now, now... if even Akasaka-san couldn't manage it, no one else could have either. You mustn't lose heart."
Saying that, Oishi gave Akasaka's shoulder an encouraging clap.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: What kind of investigation it had been was not something either could disclose or ask about, given their professions.

And yet — that Akasaka, of all people, had been forced into a position where he had to abandon it mid-course in defeat suggested readily enough that interference from a remarkably powerful figure had been at work.
Understanding that was precisely why Oishi, even cursorily, could not help but offer some comfort.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: Reluctant to interject in the exchange between the two men, Tomoe kept quiet and pretended not to hear — when she happened to glance at the clock on the wall and started.

She had a meeting arranged in the early afternoon with the chief of the prefectural police's first detective division and the others. If she didn't leave soon, factoring in possible traffic, she might actually be late.
"...... Oishi-san, I'm terribly sorry, but I think I should be going now."
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "Oh? Already? N'huhuhuh... so diligent. You could stay a little longer, you know."
"It wouldn't do to keep the division chief and the others waiting at police headquarters. ...Superintendent Akasaka, I look forward to the next occasion we might meet."
changeReason: Title changed from "Commissioner" to "Superintendent" and name references altered.

TextENGNew: "Likewise. Minaoi-san — should we ever have the chance to work together in the future, I very much hope we do."
changeReason: Character name changed from "Minai" to "Minaoi".

TextENGNew: "Thank you. ...Well then."
"Bye-bye, Auntie~!"
changeReason: Translation of "おばちゃん" changed from "old lady" to "Auntie", altering the nuance of the address term.

TextENGNew: "I'm not your aun— ow?!"
Thud.
Tomoe had been in the process of making a dignified exit — but Miyuki's blithe words threw her, and she walked straight into the half-open door with full force.
Oishi burst into roaring laughter at the sight, and Tomoe fled the room with tears in her eyes.
changeReason: Character name romanization changed from "Ooishi" to "Oishi".

TextENGNew: "...Well — what do you think of her? A good person, isn't she?"
"Yes... truly. Even among women officers, someone like her — I like her straightforwardly. How did you come to know her?"
"N'huhuhuh... Well, various things happened. She had a hard time of it when she was young. For some reason I haven't been able to look away from her since."
"A hard time...? What sort of thing?"

"...Inspector Minaoi's father was Sub-Inspector Yusuke Minaoi... a Public Security detective. Twelve years ago, there was a fire at the home of a Public Security detective and his wife... are you not aware of it? Her parents were killed by someone."
changeReason: Character name changed from "Minai" to "Minaoi" along with title changes; "Ooishi" to "Oishi".
